rationale.R1.1: The open-ended delegation asks for judgment on the thesis, gap, and contribution framing of the research passage.
rationale.R1.2: Assessing what one thinks centrally includes whether the argument from methodological fragmentation to the proposed benchmark and interpretation is coherent.
rationale.R1.3: The passage makes empirical claims from benchmark results, so checking whether the reported evidence supports the stated conclusions is central.
rationale.R1.4: The artifact explicitly claims a first standardization contribution, making novelty assessment central to the implied evaluation.
rationale.R1.7: The passage relies on named prior work and attribution, so citation and scholarship are central to evaluating the claim responsibly.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection of an open-ended opinion request must preserve uncertainty about the intended depth and available artifact context.
rationale.RX.2: The evaluator must avoid inflating the strength of conclusions beyond what the excerpt supports.
rationale.RX.3: The projection must stay within responsibility assignment rather than executing the requested opinion or critique.
rationale.RX.4: The projection should avoid enabling misleading research claims or unsupported scholarly positioning.
rationale.RX.5: The projection should preserve traceability to the delegation and artifact features that motivate each active responsibility.
